name: IRS1 null female mice had extended lifespan and delayed age-sensitive biomarkers
summary: IRS1 deletion supports downstream insulin-signaling modulation but also shows sex and insulin-resistance boundaries.
statement: Female Irs1 null mice were long-lived and showed delayed age-sensitive skin, bone, immune, and motor biomarkers despite mild insulin resistance.
time_horizon: Long-term genetic lifespan and biomarker study
quantitative_note: The source reports a female-biased lifespan effect with multiple delayed age-related biomarkers.
caveats: This is mouse genetic evidence with sex-specific benefit.
IRS1 deletion caused mild insulin resistance, complicating simple translation.
Downstream insulin-signaling genetics do not directly specify a safe human intervention.
